summary: Pi bridge-only: narrate + 2 interleaved MCP echo_contract calls, streamed live from pi markers
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

content_chunks: I will call the echo_contract tool with
I will search for the echo_contract tool in the
I will call api_bridge_echo_contract with token A_2fa62e69.
output.reassembled_message:
I will call the echo_contract tool with token A_2fa62e69.
I will search for the echo_contract tool in the MCP server.
I will call api_bridge_echo_contract with token A_2fa62e69.
I will call api_bridge_echo_contract with token B_5d8cd0c2.
PI_STREAM_A_2fa62e69 PI_STREAM_B_5d8cd0c2
output.streamed_content: I will call the echo_contract tool with token A_2fa62e69.I will search for the echo_contract tool in the MCP server.I will call api_bridge_echo_contract with token A_2fa62e69.I will call api_bridge_echo_contract with token B_5d8cd0c2.PI_STREAM_A_2fa62e69 PI_STREAM_B_5d8cd0c2

issues: Tool name granularity: pi surfaces MCP calls under the generic marker name 'mcp' (4x here) rather than the specific tool (echo_contract). The call is genuinely the MCP tool, just labelled generically — the specific name lives in the tool arguments and is not yet surfaced by our marker extension. Tracked follow-up.
agent_review.notes:
Reviewed the REASSEMBLED user-facing message (not just raw chunks). The earlier delta-garbling + run-on are FIXED: pi still streams token-level deltas that split mid-word ('...PI_STREAM_B_5d8cd' + '0c2'), but the granularity-aware reassembler concatenates them verbatim and the new message-end boundary marker separates messages, so the message a UI shows is clean:
  'I will call the echo_contract tool with token A_2fa62e69.
I will search for the echo_contract tool in the MCP server.
I will call api_bridge_echo_contract with token A_2fa62e69.
I will call api_bridge_echo_contract with token B_5d8cd0c2.
PI_STREAM_A_2fa62e69 PI_STREAM_B_5d8cd0c2'
Proper line segmentation, tokens intact, coherent narration, correct interleaving, and the final matches the work (both tokens returned = tools executed). The only open item is the generic 'mcp' tool label above.
